drugs.description: Paracetamol is a common pain reliever and fever reducer. It is one of the most widely used over-the-counter medications.
uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain (headache, toothache, muscle pain)
Relief of cold and flu symptoms

dosage.adults: 500mg to 1000mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Maximum 4000mg per day.
dosage.children: 10-15mg/kg body weight every 4-6 hours. Maximum 5 doses in 24 hours.
dosage.elderly: Lower doses may be required. Consult healthcare provider.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
Swallow tablets whole with water
Do not crush extended-release tablets
Use the measuring device provided for liquid forms
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose
Avoid alcohol while taking this medication
Check other medications for paracetamol content to avoid overdose
Seek immediate medical attention if overdose is suspected

interactions.description: May increase the effect of warfarin, increasing bleeding risk
Increases risk of liver damage
May increase risk of liver toxicity

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: If you miss a dose, take it as soon as you remember. If it's almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose to make up for a missed one.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Paracetamol is usually taken as needed, so missing a scheduled dose is generally not critical.

drugs.storage: Store below 25°C in a dry place. Keep away from children.
drugs.pregnancy: Generally considered safe during pregnancy when used as directed. Consult healthcare provider.
drugs.breastfeeding: Small amounts pass into breast milk. Generally considered safe when used occasionally.

drugs.description: Ibuprofen is an NSAID used to reduce fever, pain, and inflammation. It works by blocking the production of prostaglandins.
uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain
Treatment of arthritis and joint pain

dosage.adults: 200mg to 400mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Maximum 1200mg per day (OTC) or 3200mg (prescription).
dosage.children: 5-10mg/kg body weight every 6-8 hours. Not recommended under 6 months.
dosage.elderly: Use lowest effective dose. Increased risk of side effects.

administration: Take with food or milk to reduce stomach upset
Swallow tablets whole with a full glass of water
Do not lie down for at least 10 minutes after taking
Shake liquid suspension well before use

rare: Heart attack or stroke (with long-term use)

warnings: May increase risk of heart attack or stroke
Can cause stomach bleeding, especially in elderly
Avoid if you have a history of stomach ulcers
Do not use before or after heart bypass surgery
contraindications: Known allergy to ibuprofen or other NSAIDs

interactions.description: May reduce the cardioprotective effect of aspirin and increase bleeding risk
Increases risk of bleeding
May reduce the blood pressure lowering effect
May increase methotrexate toxicity

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's close to your next dose time, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Never take a double dose. Overdose can cause serious stomach and kidney problems.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: If using for chronic conditions, try to take doses at evenly spaced intervals.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
drugs.pregnancy: Avoid during the third trimester. Use with caution in first and second trimesters.
drugs.breastfeeding: Small amounts pass into breast milk. Generally considered safe for short-term use.

drugs.description: Amoxicillin is a penicillin-type antibiotic used to treat a wide variety of bacterial infections. It works by stopping the growth of bacteria.

dosage.adults: 250mg to 500mg every 8 hours, or 500mg to 875mg every 12 hours depending on infection.
dosage.elderly: Same as adults, but dose adjustment may be needed for kidney impairment.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
Take at evenly spaced intervals to maintain constant levels
Complete the full course even if feeling better
Shake liquid suspension well before measuring dose
warnings: Tell your doctor if you have penicillin or cephalosporin allergy
May cause severe allergic reactions in sensitive individuals
Can cause diarrhea, which may indicate a new infection
May reduce effectiveness of oral contraceptives
contraindications: History of severe allergic reaction to any beta-lactam antibiotic

interactions.description: May increase methotrexate levels and toxicity
May increase the anticoagulant effect
May reduce effectiveness of birth control pills
Increases risk of skin rash

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's almost time for the next dose, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not double the dose. Complete the full course of antibiotics as prescribed.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Missing doses or stopping early can lead to antibiotic resistance and treatment failure.

drugs.storage: Store capsules at room temperature. Refrigerate liquid suspension and discard after 14 days.
drugs.pregnancy: Generally considered safe during pregnancy. Category B.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk in small amounts. Generally considered compatible with breastfeeding.

drugs.description: Metformin is the first-line medication for type 2 diabetes. It helps control blood sugar levels by decreasing glucose production in the liver and improving insulin sensitivity.

dosage.adults: Start with 500mg once or twice daily with meals. May increase to maximum 2550mg/day in divided doses.
dosage.children: For children 10 years and older: Start with 500mg twice daily. Maximum 2000mg/day.
dosage.elderly: Start with lower doses. Monitor kidney function regularly.

administration: Take with meals to reduce stomach upset
Swallow extended-release tablets whole, do not crush or chew
Take at the same times each day for best results
Continue even if blood sugar is controlled

rare: Low blood sugar (when combined with other diabetes medications)

warnings: Stop taking before procedures with contrast dye
Risk of lactic acidosis, especially with kidney problems or alcohol use
Monitor kidney function regularly
May cause vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use
contraindications: Acute or chronic severe liver disease

interactions.description: Increases risk of lactic acidosis and low blood sugar
May cause kidney damage; stop metformin before and after procedures
Increased risk of low blood sugar
May affect kidney function and increase lactic acidosis risk

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember, unless it's close to your next scheduled dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. Monitor your blood sugar levels more frequently.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: If you frequently miss doses, consider setting daily reminders. Consistent dosing is important for blood sugar control.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
drugs.pregnancy: May be used in pregnancy for gestational diabetes under medical supervision.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk in small amounts. Generally considered safe.

drugs.description: Atorvastatin is a statin medication used to lower cholesterol and reduce the risk of heart disease. It works by blocking an enzyme needed to make cholesterol in the liver.
uses: Reduction of heart attack and stroke risk

dosage.adults: Start with 10-20mg once daily. May increase to maximum 80mg/day based on response.
dosage.children: For children 10-17 years with familial hypercholesterolemia: 10-20mg once daily.
dosage.elderly: No specific dose adjustment, but use caution.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
Take at the same time each day, preferably in the evening
Swallow tablets whole
Continue taking even if cholesterol improves
warnings: Report unexplained muscle pain or weakness immediately
Avoid grapefruit juice (increases drug levels)
Regular liver function tests may be needed
May slightly increase blood sugar levels
contraindications: Active liver disease or unexplained elevated liver enzymes

interactions.description: Increases atorvastatin levels, raising side effect risk
May increase bleeding risk
Significantly increases atorvastatin levels
Increased risk of muscle problems
May increase atorvastatin levels

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. One missed dose will not significantly affect your cholesterol control.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Try to take your statin at the same time every day. Evening dosing may be slightly more effective.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from light and moisture.
drugs.pregnancy: Contraindicated in pregnancy. May cause fetal harm.
drugs.breastfeeding: Not recommended. May pass into breast milk.

drugs.description: Amlodipine is a calcium channel blocker used to treat high blood pressure and chest pain (angina). It works by relaxing blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more easily.
uses: Prevention of heart attack and stroke

dosage.adults: Start with 5mg once daily. May increase to 10mg once daily if needed.
dosage.elderly: Start with 2.5mg once daily. Increase slowly.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
Take at the same time each day
Swallow tablets whole with water
Continue taking even if feeling well
warnings: May cause dizziness; be careful when driving
Do not stop suddenly without consulting doctor
Report swelling that worsens or breathing problems
May take 1-2 weeks for full effect
contraindications: Known allergy to amlodipine or other calcium channel blockers
Unstable angina (unless other medications are used)

interactions.description: May increase simvastatin levels; limit simvastatin to 20mg
May increase cyclosporine levels
May slightly increase amlodipine levels
May cause excessive blood pressure lowering

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. If you miss doses frequently, your blood pressure may not be controlled.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Set a daily reminder to take your medication. Consistent dosing is important for blood pressure control.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from light and moisture.
drugs.pregnancy: Use only if clearly needed. Discuss risks with your doctor.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk. Use with caution.

drugs.description: Omeprazole is a proton pump inhibitor that reduces stomach acid production. It is used to treat acid reflux, ulcers, and related conditions.

dosage.adults: 20mg once daily for most conditions. May increase to 40mg daily for severe cases.
dosage.children: Weight-based dosing: 5-10mg for children under 20kg, 10-20mg for children over 20kg.

administration: Take 30-60 minutes before a meal, preferably breakfast
Swallow capsules whole, do not crush or chew
For difficulty swallowing, capsules can be opened and contents mixed with applesauce
Complete the full course as prescribed
warnings: Long-term use may increase risk of bone fractures
May cause vitamin B12 and magnesium deficiency
Can mask symptoms of stomach cancer
Increased risk of gut infections with prolonged use
contraindications: Known allergy to omeprazole or other PPIs

interactions.description: May reduce the effectiveness of clopidogrel
May increase methotrexate levels
May increase bleeding risk
Reduced acid may decrease iron absorption

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember, unless it's almost time for your next dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. If you miss a dose, your symptoms may temporarily worsen.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: For best results, take omeprazole at the same time each day, preferably in the morning before breakfast.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture. Keep capsules in original packaging.
drugs.pregnancy: Use only if clearly needed. Limited data available.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk. Use with caution.

drugs.description: Aspirin is a versatile medication used for pain relief, fever reduction, and prevention of blood clots. Low-dose aspirin is commonly used to prevent heart attacks and strokes.
uses: Prevention of heart attacks and strokes
Prevention of blood clots after heart procedures

dosage.adults: For pain/fever: 325-650mg every 4-6 hours (max 4g/day). For heart protection: 75-100mg once daily.
dosage.children: Not recommended for children under 16 due to Reye's syndrome risk.
dosage.elderly: Use lower doses. Increased risk of bleeding.

administration: Take with food or milk to reduce stomach irritation
Take enteric-coated tablets whole, do not crush
For heart protection, take at the same time daily
Drink plenty of water with each dose
warnings: Do not give to children with viral infections (Reye's syndrome risk)
Stop taking 7 days before surgery
May cause serious stomach bleeding
Use with caution if you have asthma or nasal polyps
contraindications: Known allergy to aspirin or NSAIDs

interactions.description: May reduce aspirin's cardioprotective effect and increase bleeding risk
Significantly increases bleeding risk
May increase methotrexate toxicity
May reduce blood pressure lowering effect
Increases risk of stomach bleeding

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: For pain relief: Take when needed. For heart protection: Take as soon as you remember, then resume normal schedule.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not double the dose. If you miss your daily heart protection dose, just take the next day's dose as normal.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: If using for heart protection, consistency is important. Try to take it at the same time each day.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
drugs.pregnancy: Avoid during third trimester. Use with caution earlier. Discuss with doctor.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk. Occasional use is generally safe.

drugs.description: Cetirizine is a non-drowsy antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms. It works by blocking histamine, a substance your body makes during an allergic reaction.

dosage.adults: 10mg once daily. Can be taken in the morning or evening.
dosage.elderly: 5mg once daily, especially if kidney function is reduced.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
May be taken morning or evening
Swallow tablets whole with water
Use measuring device for liquid forms
warnings: May cause drowsiness in some people
Use caution when driving or operating machinery
Avoid alcohol as it may increase drowsiness
Inform doctor if you have kidney or liver problems
contraindications: Known allergy to cetirizine or hydroxyzine

interactions.description: May increase drowsiness and impair mental alertness
May increase sedation effects
May slightly decrease cetirizine clearance

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. Missing a dose may allow allergy symptoms to return.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: For best allergy control, take cetirizine at the same time each day.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
drugs.pregnancy: Use only if clearly needed. Discuss with your doctor.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk. Use with caution.

drugs.description: Losartan is an ARB used to treat high blood pressure and protect the kidneys in people with type 2 diabetes. It works by blocking the action of angiotensin II, which narrows blood vessels.
uses: Stroke prevention in patients with heart enlargement
Heart failure (when ACE inhibitors are not tolerated)

dosage.adults: Start with 50mg once daily. May increase to 100mg daily. Range: 25-100mg.
dosage.children: For children 6+ years: 0.7mg/kg once daily, up to 50mg.
dosage.elderly: Start with lower dose (25mg). Adjust based on response.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
Take at the same time each day
Swallow tablets whole with water
Continue taking even if blood pressure is controlled
warnings: Do not use during pregnancy (can harm the baby)
May cause dizziness; be careful when standing up
Monitor potassium levels, especially with kidney disease
Report any swelling of face, lips, or throat immediately
contraindications: Use with aliskiren in patients with diabetes

interactions.description: May cause dangerously high potassium levels
May reduce blood pressure lowering effect and affect kidney function
May increase lithium levels and toxicity
Dual blockade increases risk of low blood pressure and kidney problems

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's close to your next dose, skip the missed dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. Missing doses can cause your blood pressure to increase.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Consistent daily dosing is important. Set a reminder if you frequently miss doses.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
drugs.pregnancy: Contraindicated. Can cause fetal harm, especially in second and third trimesters.
drugs.breastfeeding: Not known if it passes into breast milk. Use with caution.

drugs.description: Spironolactone is a potassium-sparing diuretic that helps remove excess fluid from the body while preserving potassium. It is used for heart failure, high blood pressure, and conditions with excess aldosterone.

dosage.adults: 25-200mg daily in single or divided doses, depending on condition.
dosage.elderly: Start with low doses. Monitor potassium closely.

administration: Take with food to improve absorption and reduce stomach upset
Take in the morning to avoid nighttime urination
Take at consistent times each day
Swallow tablets whole
warnings: Monitor potassium levels regularly
Avoid potassium supplements and potassium-rich foods
May cause breast enlargement in men
Use with caution if you have kidney problems

interactions.description: Increased risk of high potassium levels
May cause dangerous high potassium levels
May increase digoxin levels
May reduce the diuretic effect
May alter lithium levels

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember, unless it's close to your next scheduled dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not double the dose. Skipping doses may cause fluid retention to return.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: If taking once daily, take in the morning. If you miss the morning dose, you may take it later but avoid taking it too close to bedtime.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from light and moisture.
drugs.pregnancy: Avoid during pregnancy. May cause harm to male fetus.
drugs.breastfeeding: Active metabolite passes into breast milk. Use with caution.

drugs.description: Levothyroxine is a synthetic thyroid hormone used to treat hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid). It replaces the hormone that your thyroid gland cannot produce enough of.

dosage.adults: Start with 25-50mcg daily. Adjust every 6-8 weeks based on TSH levels. Usual dose: 100-200mcg daily.
dosage.elderly: Start with very low dose (12.5-25mcg). Increase slowly.

administration: Take on an empty stomach, 30-60 minutes before breakfast
Take at the same time each day for consistent levels
Do not take with calcium or iron supplements (wait 4 hours)
Do not switch brands without consulting your doctor
warnings: Do not use for weight loss (dangerous at high doses)
Regular blood tests (TSH) are essential
Notify doctor of any heart disease before starting
Consistent daily dosing is crucial

interactions.description: Reduces absorption. Take 4 hours apart.
Reduces absorption. Take 4 hours apart.
May reduce absorption. Take 4 hours apart.
May increase warfarin effect and bleeding risk
May require dose adjustment of diabetes medications

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If you remember at bedtime, take it then.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. If you miss several doses, contact your doctor.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Levothyroxine has a long half-life, so missing occasional doses won't cause immediate problems, but consistent daily dosing is important for stable thyroid levels.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from light, heat, and moisture.
drugs.pregnancy: Safe and necessary during pregnancy. Dose may need adjustment.
drugs.breastfeeding: Small amounts pass into breast milk. Safe to use.

drugs.description: Salbutamol is a fast-acting bronchodilator used to relieve asthma symptoms and prevent exercise-induced bronchospasm. It works by relaxing the muscles in the airways.

dosage.inhaler: 1-2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed. For acute attacks: 2-4 puffs, may repeat after 20 minutes.
dosage.children: Same as adults for inhaler. Nebulizer: 2.5mg for children under 15kg.

administration: Shake inhaler well before each use
Breathe out fully before inhaling medication
Hold breath for 10 seconds after inhaling
Wait 1 minute between puffs if taking more than one
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose
If you need your inhaler more often, see your doctor
May cause low potassium with high doses
Use with caution if you have heart disease or high blood pressure

interactions.description: May reduce effectiveness of salbutamol and worsen asthma
May increase risk of low potassium
May increase side effects when used together
May increase cardiovascular effects

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Salbutamol is usually used as needed for symptoms, not on a fixed schedule.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take extra doses to make up for missed ones. Use only when you have breathing symptoms.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Always carry your rescue inhaler with you. Check expiration date regularly and replace as needed.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature. Keep away from heat and open flame. Do not puncture canister.
drugs.pregnancy: Use when benefit outweighs risk. Generally considered safe.

drugs.description: Pantoprazole is a proton pump inhibitor similar to omeprazole. It reduces stomach acid production and is used for acid-related digestive disorders.
uses: Prevention of stress ulcers in critically ill patients

dosage.adults: 40mg once daily for 4-8 weeks. May continue at 20-40mg for maintenance.
dosage.children: For children 5+ years: Weight-based dosing. 15-40kg: 20mg daily. >40kg: 40mg daily.

administration: Take 30-60 minutes before a meal
Swallow tablets whole, do not crush or chew
Can be taken with or without food, but before meals is preferred
For IV use, follow healthcare provider instructions
warnings: Long-term use may increase fracture risk
May cause low magnesium with prolonged use
Can mask symptoms of stomach cancer
May increase risk of C. difficile infection
contraindications: Known allergy to pantoprazole or other PPIs

interactions.description: May increase methotrexate levels
May increase INR and bleeding risk
Less interaction than with omeprazole, but monitor
Significantly reduces atazanavir absorption

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember, unless it's almost time for your next dose.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: For best results, take pantoprazole at the same time each day, ideally in the morning before breakfast.

drugs.storage: Store at room temperature away from moisture and heat.
drugs.pregnancy: Use only if clearly needed. Limited human data.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk. Weigh benefits and risks.

drugs.description: Azithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic used to treat various bacterial infections. It works by stopping the growth of bacteria and is known for its convenient short-course dosing.
uses: MAC infection prevention in HIV patients

dosage.adults: Varies by indication. Common: 500mg day 1, then 250mg days 2-5 (Z-Pack). Or 500mg daily for 3 days.
dosage.children: 10mg/kg on day 1, then 5mg/kg on days 2-5. Or 10mg/kg once daily for 3 days.
dosage.elderly: Same as adults. No dose adjustment needed.

administration: Can be taken with or without food
Take at the same time each day
Swallow tablets whole with water
Complete the full course even if feeling better
warnings: May cause abnormal heart rhythm in certain patients
Tell your doctor if you have heart problems or take heart medications
May cause diarrhea that could indicate a new infection
Complete the full course to prevent antibiotic resistance
contraindications: Known allergy to azithromycin or other macrolides
History of liver problems from azithromycin

interactions.description: May increase warfarin effect and bleeding risk
May reduce azithromycin absorption. Take 2 hours apart.
Increased risk of dangerous heart rhythm problems
May increase digoxin levels
May increase cyclosporine levels

missedDoseGuidance.instruction: Take the missed dose as soon as you remember. If it's almost time for your next dose, skip the missed dose and continue with your regular schedule.
missedDoseGuidance.warning: Do not take a double dose. Complete the full course of antibiotics.
missedDoseGuidance.additionalInfo: Missing doses can reduce effectiveness and contribute to antibiotic resistance. Try to take all doses on time.

drugs.storage: Store tablets at room temperature. Liquid suspension can be stored at room temperature or refrigerated.
drugs.pregnancy: Use only if clearly needed. Category B.
drugs.breastfeeding: Passes into breast milk. Use with caution.
